title: SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the sufficiency of Article-28 DPAs; see UC-22.44.2.
evidence: Daily 'scc_adequacy_freshness' writes stale-safeguard findings into compliance_summary; dashboard 'SCC inventory 90-day view'.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.46 requires appropriate safeguards; out-of-date SCCs (e.g. pre-2021 Module mismatches) are no longer appropriate and must be re-papered.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.46 (Transfers subject to safeguards) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.2: SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.45 adequacy decisions — the UC flags transfers that rely on a superseded or rescinded decision (e.g. Privacy Shield).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.45 (Transfers via adequacy decision) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.2: SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.46 (parent assurance='full'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK GDPR Art.46 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.2: SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.45 (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step. Divergence recorded — SME review should confirm scope fit.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK GDPR Art.45 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.2: SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

derivationSource.divergenceNote: UK adequacy decisions managed by ICO/UK government, not EU Commission.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When an active transfer register entry points to an SCC version or adequacy decision published more than 36 months ago, or one marked 'rescinded' in the reference list, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Entries that match an active SCC template or a current adequacy decision do NOT fire.

spl:
| inputlookup transfer_register.csv
| lookup scc_reference.csv template_id OUTPUT template_active effective_from superseded_by
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(effective_from,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where NOT template_active OR age_days>1095 OR isnotnull(superseded_by)
| table transfer_id destination template_id template_active age_days superseded_by
description: Cross-references the transfer register against the official SCC templates and the Art.45 adequacy register. Entries that rely on stale or rescinded safeguards appear immediately.
value: Prevents silent expiration of the legal basis for international transfers — the single most common finding in SCC-era enforcement.
implementation: (1) Maintain scc_reference.csv synced nightly against EDPB / Commission publications; (2) maintain transfer_register.csv as part of Art.30 ROPA; (3) run daily.
visualization: Stacked bar of active vs stale vs rescinded, table of findings, single-value '% of transfers with fresh safeguards'.
knownFalsePositives: Transfer entries mid-migration (with `migration=in_flight`) should be allow-listed by a start/end date.
grandmaExplanation: We help you prevent silent expiration of the legal basis for international transfers — the single most common finding in SCC-era enforcement — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain scc_reference.csv synced nightly against EDPB / Commission publications; (2) maintain transfer_register.csv as part of Art.30 ROPA; (3) run daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup transfer_register.csv
| lookup scc_reference.csv template_id OUTPUT template_active effective_from superseded_by
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(effective_from,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where NOT template_active OR age_days>1095 OR isnotnull(superseded_by)
| table transfer_id destination template_id template_active age_days superseded_by
```

Understanding this SPL

**SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector** — Prevents silent expiration of the legal basis for international transfers — the single most common finding in SCC-era enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where NOT template_active OR age_days>1095 OR isnotnull(superseded_by)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **SCC / adequacy decision reference freshness — stale-safeguard detector**): table transfer_id destination template_id template_active age_days superseded_by


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar of active vs stale vs rescinded, table of findings, single-value '% of transfers with fresh safeguards'.
